titleStmt.title: Letter from John Robert O'Connell to Sir Matthew Nathan, 1 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Robert O'Connell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Sir John Robert O'Connell to Sir Matthew Nathan, under-secretary for Ireland. O'Connell (1868-1943) was a solicitor applying for the chief crown solicitor position. Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 until 1916. In 1916 he faced criticism for failing to be aware of and to prevent the outbreak of the Easter Rising on 24 April. He resigned shortly afterwards. In this letter O'Connell mentions that he is sending references from Cardinal Michael Logue (1840-1924), the Archbishop of Armagh, and from John Harty (1867-1946), the Archbishop of Cashel in reference to his application for the post of chief crown solicitor. O'Connell adds that he has obtained a reference from John Mangan (1853-1917), the Bishop of Kerry, to the same effect.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/19

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
34, KILDARE STREET
DUBLIN.
3rd April 1916 Dear Sir Matthew Nathan In further reference
to my letter to you of
27th ulto I send you
herewith letter from His
Eminence Cardinal
Logue, Archbishop of
Armagh and from His
Grace Most Revd Dr
Harty Archbishop of
Cashel in reference to my
application for the office
of Chief Crown Solicitor
for Ireland. I may also
add that I have received
a very strong letter from the
Most Revd Dr Mangan
Bishop of Kerry to the same
effect I remain
Dear Sir Matthew
Yours Sincerely John Robert O' Connell
The Right Honourable
G.C.V.O. K.E.V.O.
Sir Matthew Nathan The Castle Dublin
